description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a dark, metallic finish and a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, crescent-shaped blades on either side of a central mounting, each blade curving outward with pointed tips and a pronounced central ridge. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of a dark wood or composite material, capped at both ends with metallic, pointed pommels. The central grip section is reinforced and slightly thicker, likely for improved handling and durability. The overall aesthetic is imposing and functional, suitable for display or use in ceremonial or practical contexts. No visible markings or decorative engravings are present, giving the axe a utilitarian and intimidating appearance.
description: A double-headed battle axe with large, dark crescent blades and a straight, reinforced handle. The design is robust, symmetrical, and utilitarian.
description_view_0: The axe is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the double blades and the straight handle.
description_view_1: The front view reveals the full width of the two large, crescent-shaped blades and the central reinforced grip.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, mirroring view 0, highlighting the slim silhouette of the axe.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back of the double blades and the handle, similar to the front but with no visible markings.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle made of dark wood or composite, capped with pointed metallic pommels, no visible curvature or grip texture.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with pointed tips and a pronounced central ridge, symmetrical and robust with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted over the handle's central section.
